canonical_import: from lazybridge import Agent, LLMEngine, tool

canonical_patterns.sequential_plan: agent = Agent(engine=Plan(Step('research'), Step('write')), tools=[research_agent, write_agent], name='pipeline')
canonical_patterns.parallel_fanout:
fan = Agent.parallel(researcher, analyst)
env = fan('topic')           # one Envelope
env.text()                      # labelled-text join across branches
env.payload                     # list[Envelope] for typed access
canonical_patterns.chain: pipe = Agent.chain(researcher, writer)   # linear Plan shortcut
canonical_patterns.agent_as_tool: tools=[other_agent]              # auto-wrapped; other_agent.name is the tool name
canonical_patterns.mcp_server: tools=[MCP.stdio('fs', command='npx', args=[...], allow=['fs.read_*'])]   # allow= REQUIRED since 0.7.9
canonical_patterns.async_entry_point: result = await agent.run('do the thing'); result.text()

envelope_contract.payload: result.payload -> T — typed payload when output= was set; otherwise str
envelope_contract.error: result.error -> Exception | None — propagated runtime failure
envelope_contract.metadata: result.metadata.cost_usd / input_tokens / output_tokens / latency_ms / model / provider / run_id / nested_*

avoid: LazyTool — renamed to tool() factory in 0.7.x
LazyContext — replaced by sentinels / Store / Memory in 0.7.x
mode="auto" — Tool.__init__ rejects this since 0.7.9; pass mode="signature", "llm", or "hybrid"
Agent.from_chain / from_engine / from_model / from_plan / from_parallel — all five deleted in 0.7.9 (alias-only); use Agent(engine=...) or Agent.chain / Agent.parallel
AgentRuntimeConfig / ResilienceConfig / ObservabilityConfig — deleted in 0.7.9; pass the fields as flat kwargs on Agent(...)
Writing JSON schemas by hand for annotated Python functions — tool() introspects type hints automatically
MCP.stdio(...) without allow= or deny= — deny-by-default since 0.7.9, raises ValueError
asyncio.run(main()) in quickstarts — Agent.__call__ auto-detects event loops
lazybridge.external_tools.report_builder — extracted to selvaz/LazyReport in 0.7.9; install lazybridge-reports separately

tools_vs_native_tools.tools=: Tools the LazyBridge runtime executes locally — Python functions, sub-Agents, MCP servers, schema-backed Tool instances.
tools_vs_native_tools.native_tools=: Provider-side tools the LLM invokes via the vendor's native API (web search, code execution, etc.).  Pass a NativeTool enum value, e.g. native_tools=[NativeTool.WEB_SEARCH].  Dangerous tools (CODE_EXECUTION, COMPUTER_USE) require allow_dangerous_native_tools=True.

default_model_advice: Use Agent.from_provider('anthropic', tier='top') in quickstarts so the example stays current as providers ship new SKUs.  Pin a specific model id (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7') only when the example tests a model-specific behaviour.
